---
title: "Splatter test of BBP/RMT depth threshold"
output:
  html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_depth: 2
    theme: readable
params:
  out_dir: "outputs/exploration/splatter_bbp_depth_threshold"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE, fig.retina = 2)
local_lib <- normalizePath(".rlib/R", mustWork = FALSE)
if (dir.exists(local_lib)) .libPaths(c(local_lib, .libPaths()))
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(knitr)

out_dir <- params$out_dir
replicate_metrics <- read.csv(file.path(out_dir, "replicate_metrics.csv"))
setting_summary <- read.csv(file.path(out_dir, "setting_summary.csv"))
threshold_summary <- read.csv(file.path(out_dir, "threshold_summary.csv"))
grid <- read.csv(file.path(out_dir, "simulation_grid.csv"))
fmt <- function(x, digits = 3) signif(x, digits)
```

## Executive summary

This report tests the prediction that, once the number of cells is large enough, the depth threshold for spectral detection is governed primarily by

\[
N_{\min} \propto \frac{p_{\mathrm{eff}}}{\rho m},
\]

rather than by `n` directly. The simulations use `splatter::splatSimulateGroups()` with two groups, fixed `p_eff = 2000`, and differential expression only in the rare group.

```{r top-line}
overall <- setting_summary %>%
  summarise(
    settings = n(),
    replicate_runs = sum(reps),
    median_depth_error = median(abs(mean_total_umi - N) / N, na.rm = TRUE),
    min_scale = min(scale_x),
    max_scale = max(scale_x)
  )
kable(overall, digits = 3, caption = "Simulation coverage and depth calibration")
```

Detection is scored three ways: maximum one-dimensional AUC over PCs 1--10, maximum absolute correlation between the rare-cell label and PCs 1--10, and k-means ARI on the top 10 PCs. The main binary detection call below uses `max AUC > 0.8`; ARI summaries are retained in the source tables.

## Simulation design

- `splatSimulateGroups()` with `group.prob = c(1 - rho, rho)`.
- `nGenes = p_eff = 2000`.
- Expected cell depth is controlled through Splat log-normal library sizes with `lib.scale = 0.15` and `lib.loc = log(N) - lib.scale^2 / 2`.
- Rare-group markers use `de.prob = c(0, m / p_eff)`, `de.downProb = c(0, 0)`, and a fixed moderate up-regulation factor centered at `log(2)`.
- Background-group differential expression probability is set to zero, so the background group remains close to the base population.
- Counts are size-factor normalized by total UMI and transformed with `log1p`.
- PCA is computed with truncated SVD on centered cell-by-gene log-normalized expression.

The report profile is intentionally smaller than the full Cartesian grid, but every retained setting has the requested replicate count. The full grid can be run with:

```sh
Rscript scripts/exploration/splatter_bbp_depth_threshold.R --profile=full --reps=20
```

## Heatmap over n and N

```{r heatmap, out.width='100%'}
knitr::include_graphics(file.path(out_dir, "heatmap_detection_probability.png"))
```

For fixed `rho = 0.05` and `m = 50`, the transition is primarily vertical in `N`: increasing depth creates reliable detection. Increasing `n` helps at the low end but is not a substitute for enough per-cell marker information.

## Collapse by rho m N / p_eff

```{r collapse, out.width='100%'}
knitr::include_graphics(file.path(out_dir, "collapse_detection_probability.png"))
```

The collapse variable \(\rho m N / p_{\mathrm{eff}}\) is the reciprocal of the predicted depth scale, up to a constant. A successful BBP/RMT-style scaling argument predicts that large-`n` curves should align when plotted against this axis. Small-`n` departures are expected because finite-sample PCA has extra variability before the asymptotic regime is reached.

## Threshold scaling

```{r threshold, out.width='100%'}
knitr::include_graphics(file.path(out_dir, "threshold_scaling.png"))
```

Here \(N_{\min}\) is estimated as the smallest simulated `N` for which detection probability reaches at least 0.5. Because `N` is evaluated on a coarse grid, these are interval-censored threshold estimates rather than precise transition points.

```{r threshold-table}
threshold_table <- threshold_summary %>%
  filter(!is.na(N_min_auc)) %>%
  arrange(n, rho, m) %>%
  mutate(theory_x = fmt(theory_x, 4)) %>%
  select(n, rho, m, theory_x, N_min_auc, N_min_either)
kable(head(threshold_table, 30), caption = "Estimated thresholds; first 30 rows")
```

## Saturation below threshold

```{r saturation, out.width='100%'}
knitr::include_graphics(file.path(out_dir, "saturation_low_depth.png"))
```

The saturation panel fixes low depths and varies `n`. If a setting is below the spectral threshold, the AUC can improve slightly with more cells, but the expected signature is a plateau below reliable detection rather than monotone rescue by `n` alone.

## Metric agreement

```{r metric-agreement}
metric_summary <- setting_summary %>%
  summarise(
    median_detection_auc = median(detection_probability_auc, na.rm = TRUE),
    median_detection_ari = median(detection_probability_ari, na.rm = TRUE),
    median_mean_max_abs_cor = median(mean_max_abs_cor, na.rm = TRUE),
    median_gap_1_2 = median(mean_gap_1_2, na.rm = TRUE),
    median_gap_1_bulk_edge = median(mean_gap_1_bulk_edge, na.rm = TRUE)
  )
kable(metric_summary, digits = 3, caption = "Secondary metric summary across settings")
```

```{r metric-scatter, fig.width=7, fig.height=5}
ggplot(setting_summary, aes(mean_max_auc, mean_ari, color = detection_probability_auc)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.75) +
  scale_color_viridis_c(limits = c(0, 1)) +
  labs(
    title = "AUC and clustering recovery move together, but are not identical",
    x = "Mean max AUC over PCs 1--10",
    y = "Mean k-means ARI on top 10 PCs",
    color = "P(AUC > 0.8)"
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 12)
```

## Source files

The generated source tables are:

- `simulation_grid.csv`
- `replicate_metrics.csv`
- `setting_summary.csv`
- `threshold_summary.csv`

The analysis script is `scripts/exploration/splatter_bbp_depth_threshold.R`.
